target_customer.description: High-net-worth professionals: business owners, executives, and medical professionals with $500K+ in investable assets
target_customer.demographics: Ages 45–65, household income $300K+, business owners and senior professionals in the Southeast US
target_customer.psychographics: Value trust and competence over price, seeking a long-term advisor relationship, risk-aware, time-poor
target_customer.primary_pain: Distrust of financial advisors who prioritise commissions over outcomes — want a fiduciary who treats them as intelligent adults

primary_offer: Comprehensive financial planning and investment management for high-net-worth individuals and families

revenue_streams.name: AUM-based management fees (1% of AUM annually)
Project-based engagements — business exit planning, estate structuring

operations.peak_hours_or_seasons: Q1 tax season, Q4 year-end planning
operations.primary_delivery_method: In-person meetings and virtual video calls

current_goal: Attract and convert more high-net-worth professionals — increase AUM and revenue per client without proportionally increasing headcount
current_challenges: Generic positioning — website and marketing do not differentiate from large national RIAs
Booking friction — prospects must call to schedule; no online booking or self-serve qualification
Slow proposal process — proposals take 3–5 business days and close at lower rate than industry average
No formal referral programme — referrals happen organically but are not systematically captured or rewarded
No clear premium entry-point service — all prospects routed to same full-service engagement regardless of readiness
constraints_known: Lead advisor capacity — firm can handle approximately 80 clients at current service level before quality degrades
No CRM-driven pipeline — lead follow-up is manual and inconsistent

competitors.name: Merrill Lynch / wirehouse advisors
competitors.positioning: Brand trust, broad product shelf, institutional resources

competitors.name: Robo-advisors (Betterment, Schwab Intelligent Portfolios)
competitors.estimated_market_share: Relevant only at lower AUM tiers

available_data_sources.data_available: Client list, AUM per client, tenure, service tier
Traffic, session duration, bounce rate, top pages, contact form submissions
Inbound inquiries, proposal requests, follow-up threads

missing_critical_data: Average time from first contact to signed engagement
Client churn rate and reasons for departure
Referral source tracking — which clients refer and how often
Proposal acceptance rate and reasons for decline
